Title: Simultaneous Knockout of Tk1-SST and Tk1-FFT via CRISPR/Cas9 Enhances the Natural Rubber Accumulation in Taraxacum kok-saghyz

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: line 1152; mutant lines ssff-10, ssff-17, ssff-36 derived from 1152
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CRISPR/Cas9 editing construct: Cas9 driven by ubiquitin promoter; sgRNAs driven by Arabidopsis U3/U6 promoters
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Strongly expressed in WT roots at ~200 DAC; severely suppressed in double mutants
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Root; WT roots at ~200 DAC detectable by qRT-PCR and western blot, absent/suppressed in 1-sst1-fft mutants
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Fructosyltransferase; glycoside hydrolase family 32-type fructan biosynthetic enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Reserve carbohydrate; supports germination/regrowth; osmotic regulation; contributes to drought and cold tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Initial committed step of inulin/fructan formation from sucrose
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers a fructosyl unit from one sucrose molecule to another to generate the initial fructan 1-kestose and glucose, initiating inulin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms early inulin precursor and initiates target-product biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Established key control step for inulin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Knockout of Tk1-SST together with Tk1-FFT abolishes inulin biosynthesis, markedly increases root sucrose, and promotes >2-fold higher natural rubber accumulation in TKS roots
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 double knockout; Hi-TOM sequencing; qRT-PCR; western blot; HPLC inulin quantification and DP analysis; sugar profiling; RNA-seq; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Tk1-SST is essential for inulin biosynthesis in TKS roots. 2. Simultaneous knockout with Tk1-FFT reduced inulin to ~1% of WT and sharply lowered DP. 3. Blocking this step increased sucrose availability and was associated with strongly increased natural rubber and terpenoid accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Greenhouse growth under 16 h light/8 h dark at 23°C; roots sampled at ~120, ~200, and ~300 DAC
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (Illumina HiSeq); WGS; GC/MS sugar metabolomics; UPLC-MS/MS terpenoid metabolomics; GSA accession CRA026283 for WGS raw data
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value target for CRISPR knockout to redirect carbon flux from inulin to natural rubber; best deployed with Tk1-FFT knockout as a double mutant for NR-rich TKS breeding
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Eliminates inulin, a reserve carbohydrate implicated in winter dormancy and abiotic stress protection; root/shoot ratio decreased
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Double knockout is the first inulin synthesis-deficient mutant reported in plants according to the authors; Tk1-SST and Tk1-FFT are tightly linked (~29.74 kb apart) and co-segregate frequently

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: line 1152; mutant lines ssff-10, ssff-17, ssff-36 derived from 1152
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CRISPR/Cas9 editing construct: Cas9 driven by ubiquitin promoter; sgRNAs driven by Arabidopsis U3/U6 promoters
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Strongly expressed in WT roots at ~200 DAC; severely suppressed in double mutants
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Root; WT roots at ~200 DAC detectable by qRT-PCR and western blot, absent/suppressed in 1-sst1-fft mutants
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Fructosyltransferase; glycoside hydrolase family 32-type fructan biosynthetic enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Reserve carbohydrate; supports germination/regrowth; osmotic regulation; contributes to drought and cold tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: 1-kestose and other fructans; sucrose/fructose can act as acceptor-related substrates in Asteraceae fructan metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers fructosyl units between fructan molecules to elongate inulin chains and increase degree of polymerization
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Established key control step for inulin polymerization
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Knockout of Tk1-FFT together with Tk1-SST abolishes inulin synthesis/elongation, elevates sucrose, and enhances natural rubber accumulation in roots
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 double knockout; Hi-TOM sequencing; qRT-PCR; western blot; HPLC inulin quantification and DP analysis; sugar profiling; RNA-seq; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Tk1-FFT is essential for inulin polymer elongation in TKS. 2. Simultaneous knockout with Tk1-SST reduced inulin DP from 11.5 in WT to 2.1-3.3 in mutants and nearly eliminated inulin. 3. Carbon reallocation following loss of Tk1-FFT contributes to increased natural rubber accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Greenhouse growth under 16 h light/8 h dark at 23°C; roots sampled at ~120, ~200, and ~300 DAC
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (Illumina HiSeq); WGS; GC/MS sugar metabolomics; UPLC-MS/MS terpenoid metabolomics; GSA accession CRA026283 for WGS raw data
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value CRISPR knockout target for reducing inulin and redirecting carbon to NR, especially in combination with Tk1-SST knockout
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Eliminates inulin reserve pool and may affect stress buffering/dormancy functions; root/shoot ratio decreased
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors note prior single-gene Tk1-fft mutants showed limited or genotype-dependent effects; the double homozygous knockout provided much stronger NR enhancement
